2603.19066.title: Parallelograms Strike Back: LLMs Generate Better Analogies than People
2603.19066.paper_summary: Four-term word analogies (A:B::C:D) are classically modeled geometrically as ''parallelograms,'' yet recent work suggests this model poorly captures how humans produce analogies, with simple local-similarity heuristics often providing a better account (Peterson et al., 2020). But does the parallelogram model fail because it is a bad model of analogical relations, or because people are not very good at generating relation-preserving analogies? We compared human and large language model (LLM) analogy completions on the same set of analogy problems from (Peterson et al., 2020). We find that LLM-generated analogies are reliably judged as better than human-generated ones, and are also more closely aligned with the parallelogram structure in a distributional embedding space (GloVe). Crucially, we show that the improvement over human analogies was driven by greater parallelogram alignment and reduced reliance on accessible words rather than enhanced sensitivity to local similarity. Moreover, the LLM advantage is driven not by uniformly superior responses by LLMs, but by humans producing a long tail of weak completions: when only modal (most frequent) responses by both systems are compared, the LLM advantage disappears. However, greater parallelogram alignment and lower word frequency continue to predict which LLM completions are rated higher than those of humans. Overall, these results suggest that the parallelogram model is not a poor account of word analogy. Rather, humans may often fail to produce completions that satisfy this relational constraint, whereas LLMs do so more consistently.
2603.19066.paper_summary_zh: 四項詞語類比 (A:B::C:D) 在傳統上被幾何地建模為「平行四邊形」，然而最近的研究表明這一模型無法很好地捕捉人類生成類比的方式，簡單的局部相似性啟發式往往能提供更好的解釋 (Peterson et al., 2020)。但平行四邊形模型的失敗是因為它對類比關係的建模不佳，還是因為人類在生成保持關係的類比方面並不擅長？我們比較了人類和大型語言模型 (LLM) 在同一組類比問題上的類比完成 (Peterson et al., 2020)。我們發現LLM生成的類比在評價上被可靠地判定為優於人類生成的類比，並且在分佈嵌入空間 (GloVe) 中與平行四邊形結構的對齊程度更高。關鍵是，我們顯示出對人類類比的改進是由於更大的平行四邊形對齊和對可接近詞彙的依賴減少，而不是對局部相似性的敏感性提高。此外，LLM的優勢並不是由於LLM的回應始終優於人類，而是因為人類生成了一長串弱的完成：當僅比較兩個系統的模式（最頻繁）回應時，LLM的優勢消失。然而，更大的平行四邊形對齊和較低的詞頻仍然預測哪些LLM的完成被評價高於人類的完成。總體而言，這些結果表明平行四邊形模型並不是對詞語類比的糟糕解釋。相反，人類可能經常無法生成滿足這一關係約束的完成，而LLM則能更一致地做到這一點。
2603.19066.author: Qiawen Ella Liu et.al.
2603.19066.authors: Qiawen Ella Liu, Raja Marjieh, Jian-Qiao Zhu, Adele E. Goldberg, Thomas L. Griffiths
